title: Iced Argentinian Winey Pilaf
description: A hearty, comforting pilaf perfect for a sick day. This recipe simmers the pilaf in red wine for a rich, complex flavor.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large salt-block skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the ground black pepper and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the uncooked white rice and cook for 2 minutes, until lightly toasted.
Add the red wine and chicken broth, and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet, and simmer for 20 minutes, until the liquid has been absorbed and the rice is tender.
Stir in the iced water and serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley.
Let the pilaf cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Reheat the pilaf over low heat, adding a splash of iced water if necessary to achieve the desired consistency.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
